titles.title: BayCauRETM: R package for Bayesian Causal Inference for Recurrent Event Outcomes

institution.institution_name: Department of Biostatistics, Brown University, Providence, RI, United States
Department of Biostatistics, Brown University, Providence, RI, United States
Department of Biostatistics, Brown University, Providence, RI, United States

citation.article_title: A Bayesian framework for causal analysis of recurrent events with timing misalignment
citation.author: Oganisian
citation.unstructured_citation: Oganisian, A., Girard, A., Steingrimsson, J. A., & Moyo, P. (2024). A Bayesian framework for causal analysis of recurrent events with timing misalignment. Biometrics, 80(4), ujae145. https://doi.org/10.1093/biomtc/ujae145

citation.article_title: Joint modelling of event counts and survival times
citation.author: Cowling
citation.journal_title: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C (Applied Statistics)
citation.unstructured_citation: Cowling, B. J., Hutton, J. L., & Shaw, J. E. H. (2006). Joint modelling of event counts and survival times. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C (Applied Statistics), 55(1), 31–39. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1467-9876.2005.00529.x

citation.article_title: Multi-state models for panel data: The msm package for R
citation.author: Jackson
citation.unstructured_citation: Jackson, C. (2011). Multi-state models for panel data: The msm package for R. Journal of Statistical Software, 38, 1–28. https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v038.i08

citation.article_title: Regression modeling for recurrent events possibly with an informative terminal event using R package reReg
citation.author: Chiou
citation.unstructured_citation: Chiou, S. H., Xu, G., Yan, J., & Huang, C.-Y. (2023). Regression modeling for recurrent events possibly with an informative terminal event using R package reReg. Journal of Statistical Software, 105(5), 1–34. https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v105.i05

citation.article_title: Multi-state models for event history analysis
citation.author: Andersen
citation.unstructured_citation: Andersen, P. K., & Keiding, N. (2002). Multi-state models for event history analysis. Statistical Methods in Medical Research, 11(2), 91–115. https://doi.org/10.1191/0962280202SM276ra

citation.article_title: Marginal regression models for recurrent and terminal events
citation.author: Ghosh
citation.unstructured_citation: Ghosh, D., & Lin, D. Y. (2002). Marginal regression models for recurrent and terminal events. Statistica Sinica, 663–688.

citation.article_title: Estimating treatment effects on the marginal recurrent event mean in the presence of a terminating event
citation.author: Schaubel
citation.unstructured_citation: Schaubel, D. E., & Zhang, M. (2010). Estimating treatment effects on the marginal recurrent event mean in the presence of a terminating event. Lifetime Data Analysis, 16(4), 451–477. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10985-009-9149-x

citation.article_title: Doubly robust estimation and causal inference for recurrent event data
citation.author: Su
citation.unstructured_citation: Su, C.-L., Steele, R., & Shrier, I. (2020). Doubly robust estimation and causal inference for recurrent event data. Statistics in Medicine, 39(17), 2324–2338. https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.8541

citation.article_title: Causal inference with recurrent and competing events
citation.author: Janvin
citation.unstructured_citation: Janvin, M., Young, J. G., Ryalen, P. C., & Stensrud, M. J. (2024). Causal inference with recurrent and competing events. Lifetime Data Analysis, 30(1), 59–118. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10985-023-09594-8

citation.author: Carpenter
citation.unstructured_citation: Carpenter, B., Gelman, A., Hoffman, M. D., Lee, D., Goodrich, B., Betancourt, M., Brubaker, M., Guo, J., Li, P., & Riddell, A. (2017). Stan: A probabilistic programming language. Journal of Statistical Software, 76(1), 1–32. https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v076.i01
